     3. Kanban system (fully implemented)
     4. Agent workspaces (per-task isolation)
     5. Claude Code integration (complete)

     • Architecture diagram (ASCII)
     • Features mapping (implementation + extension)
     • Integration pattern (how Claude Code works)
     • Workspace structure (directory layout)
     • Configuration hierarchy (precedence)
     • Database essentials (core tables)
     • MCP tools organization
     • Step-by-step: Extending Fulcrum for Omega
     • Performance & scaling notes
     • Security considerations
     • Testing & development
     • Quick commands reference
     • One-liner summary
   Best For: Developers, reference while coding
   Reading Time: 25-35 minutes

   Purpose: Complete technical deep-dive (MAIN DOCUMENT)

     1. Architecture Overview
     2. Kanban System Implementation
     3. Agent Workspaces - Per-Task Isolation
     4. Claude Code Integration
     5. OpenCode Integration

     • Data flow walkthroughs
   Best For: Comprehensive understanding, implementation planning
   Reading Time: 60-90 minutes

================================================================================
HOW TO USE THIS RESEARCH
================================================================================

SCENARIO 1: I need a quick overview (15 min)
  → Read: README_FULCRUM_RESEARCH.md "Key Findings at a Glance"
  → Read: FULCRUM_KEY_FINDINGS.txt sections 1-3

SCENARIO 2: I'm designing Omega integration (1-2 hours)
  → Read: README_FULCRUM_RESEARCH.md (full)
  → Read: FULCRUM_QUICK_REFERENCE.md (full)
  → Skim: FULCRUM_TECHNICAL_ANALYSIS.md sections 4-8

SCENARIO 3: I'm implementing Omega (3-4 days)
  → Read: FULCRUM_QUICK_REFERENCE.md (reference)
  → Read: FULCRUM_TECHNICAL_ANALYSIS.md (full, especially sections 4-8)
  → Refer to: FULCRUM_KEY_FINDINGS.txt section 8 (extension checklist)

SCENARIO 4: I'm doing architecture review (2-3 hours)
  → Read: README_FULCRUM_RESEARCH.md
  → Read: FULCRUM_TECHNICAL_ANALYSIS.md sections 1, 9-11
  → Review: FULCRUM_QUICK_REFERENCE.md "Architecture Diagram"

SCENARIO 5: I need specific answers
  → Check: README_FULCRUM_RESEARCH.md "By Task" section
  → Then read appropriate section from other documents

================================================================================
DOCUMENT READING ORDER
================================================================================

For First-Time Readers:
  1. README_FULCRUM_RESEARCH.md (15 min) - Get oriented
  2. FULCRUM_QUICK_REFERENCE.md (25 min) - Understand basic structure
  3. FULCRUM_KEY_FINDINGS.txt (20 min) - Deep dive into details
  4. FULCRUM_TECHNICAL_ANALYSIS.md (as needed) - Reference specific sections

For Quick Lookup:
  • Find section in README_FULCRUM_RESEARCH.md "By Task"
  • Jump to relevant document section
  • Use Ctrl+F to search for specific terms

For Implementation:
  • Start with FULCRUM_QUICK_REFERENCE.md "Extending Fulcrum for Omega"
  • Reference FULCRUM_TECHNICAL_ANALYSIS.md OpenCode implementation
  • Consult FULCRUM_KEY_FINDINGS.txt section 8 (integration checklist)

================================================================================
QUICK ANSWERS TO COMMON QUESTIONS
================================================================================

Q: What is Fulcrum?
A: AI-powered command center for orchestrating multiple agents (Claude Code,
   OpenCode, Omega) across isolated tasks in parallel. Version: 4.7.1
   → See: KEY_FINDINGS section 1

Q: How does Fulcrum provide per-agent workspaces?
A: Three types: Worktrees (git-isolated), Scratch (filesystem-isolated), 
   Manual (tracking-only). Uses dtach for persistent terminal sessions.
   → See: KEY_FINDINGS section 4, QUICK_REFERENCE "Workspace Structure"

Q: How does Claude Code integration work?
A: Plugin hooks fire on idle/submit → Run `fulcrum current-task {status}` 
   → CLI detects task from worktree → HTTP call → Task status updated
   → See: KEY_FINDINGS section 5, ANALYSIS section 4

Q: How do I add Omega as a new agent?
A: Create OmegaChatService, add provider check in routes, add 'omega' to 
   schema enums, create plugin/hooks for status sync. ~1000 LOC, 3-4 days.
   → See: QUICK_REFERENCE "Extending Fulcrum for Omega", ANALYSIS section 12

Q: What MCP tools are available?
A: 100+ tools across 20 categories (tasks, projects, filesystem, memory, 
   calendar, notifications, messaging, etc.) with deferred loading.
   → See: KEY_FINDINGS section 7, ANALYSIS section 7

Q: How is the database structured?
A: SQLite with 30+ tables (tasks, terminals, chat sessions, projects, 
   calendars, messaging, etc.) using Drizzle ORM with migrations.
   → See: ANALYSIS section 8, KEY_FINDINGS section 12

Q: What's the tech stack?
A: React 19 + TanStack Router (frontend), Hono on Bun (backend), SQLite + 
   Drizzle ORM (database), xterm.js + dtach (terminals), MCP + 100+ tools
   → See: KEY_FINDINGS section 10, QUICK_REFERENCE (top)

Q: Is Fulcrum extensible for new agents?
A: Yes, explicitly designed for multiple agents. Claude Code and OpenCode 
   are proof-of-concept. Follow OpenCode pattern for Omega.
   → See: ANALYSIS section 12, KEY_FINDINGS section 8

================================================================================
KEY INSIGHTS
================================================================================

1. Fulcrum was EXPLICITLY DESIGNED for multiple agents
   → Claude Code and OpenCode are not special cases
   → Follow the same pattern for Omega

2. Terminal isolation is the core innovation
   → Git worktrees provide branch isolation
   → Scratch directories provide filesystem isolation
   → dtach provides session persistence
   → This is TTY-agnostic (works for any shell-based agent)

3. MCP tools are the integration interface
   → 100+ pre-built tools, all agent-agnostic
   → Omega gets full access without modifications
   → Just implement ChatService + status tracker

4. Status sync is pluggable
   → Claude: plugin hooks
   → OpenCode: SDK events
   → Omega: can use either approach
   → All methods call same CLI endpoint: `fulcrum current-task {status}`

5. Configuration is hierarchical
   → Task > Repository > Project > Global defaults
   → Enables both flexibility and consistency
   → Omega can follow same pattern

6. No schema restructuring needed for new agents
   → Just add to agent/provider enums
   → Add new session ID field if needed
   → Everything else works automatically

7. No context bloat with deferred tool loading
   → Only 8 core tools in context by default
   → Others discovered via search_tools
   → Critical for token efficiency

================================================================================
NEXT STEPS FOR OMEGA INTEGRATION
================================================================================

PHASE 1: Design (1 day)
  ☐ Review FULCRUM_QUICK_REFERENCE.md
  ☐ Study OpenCode integration (ANALYSIS section 5)
  ☐ Design OmegaProvider interface
  ☐ Create integration checklist
  → Estimated effort: 4-6 hours

PHASE 2: Prototype (1 day)
  ☐ Create OmegaChatService (echo OpenCodeChatService)
  ☐ Add provider check in assistant routes
  ☐ Test basic message passing
  → Estimated effort: 4-6 hours

PHASE 3: Status Tracking (1 day)
  ☐ Implement event tracking (mirror OpenCode pattern)
  ☐ Create status change logic
  ☐ Test status updates
  → Estimated effort: 4-6 hours

PHASE 4: Integration & Testing (1 day)
  ☐ Add to database schema
  ☐ Update settings
  ☐ Create plugin/hooks
  ☐ End-to-end testing
  → Estimated effort: 4-6 hours

TOTAL ESTIMATED EFFORT: 3-4 days
